S.n: From Zero to AI Power User

B.x: From Zero to AI Power User

S.n: Today We Cover Four Core Areas

I.title: Intro
I.x: What AI copilots are and why they matter

I.title: Basics
I.x: Prompt structure, anatomy, and best practices

S.n: A Prompt is Your Instruction to the AI

B.x: A prompt is any text input you send to an AI model. It's the primary interface — your natural language command. The quality of your output is directly shaped by the quality of your input.

You are a helpful writing assistant. I need to write a professional email to a client explaining that our project deadline has been pushed back by two weeks due to unexpected technical issues. The tone should be apologetic but confident. Please write a 3-paragraph email that acknowledges the delay, explains the reason briefly without over-explaining, and ends with a clear revised timeline and a commitment to quality.
Notice how this prompt includes a role, a task, a tone requirement, and a format constraint — all in one instruction.
B.title: Key Insight

S.n: Every Strong Prompt Has Three Core Parts

B.x: Break any prompt into three reusable components. Master these and you can construct prompts for any situation.

You are a {{role}}.

Your task is to {{task}}.

Format your response as a {{format}}.

I: {{role}} — Sets the AI's persona and expertise level
{{task}} — Describes exactly what you want the AI to do
{{format}} — Specifies how the output should be structured

S.n: Challenge 1 — Write Your Very First Prompt

B.title: Write Your First Prompt
B.desc: Using the Role + Task + Format anatomy, write a prompt that asks the AI to help you draft a short bio for a LinkedIn profile. Think about what role to assign, what the task is, and how you want the output formatted.

B.x: Don't overthink it! Your first prompt doesn't need to be perfect. The goal is to practice using the three-part structure.
B.title: Remember

S.n: Three Tips That Separate Good Prompts from Great Ones

B.x: Be specific about length. Instead of 'write something short', say 'write exactly 3 sentences' or 'keep it under 100 words'. Vague length instructions produce inconsistent results.
B.title: Tip 1 — Specify Length Precisely

B.x: Give the AI an example of what good looks like. Add 'Here's an example of the style I want: [example]' and the model will pattern-match to your sample rather than guessing.
B.title: Tip 2 — Show, Don't Just Tell

B.x: If the output isn't quite right, don't start over — iterate. Say 'That's good, but make it more formal' or 'Shorten that by 50%'. Conversation history is your superpower.
B.title: Tip 3 — Iterate, Don't Restart
Iterate and Refine
B.desc: Take your LinkedIn bio prompt from Challenge 1 and run it at least 3 times with different refinements. Try changing the tone (casual → professional → humorous), adjusting the length, and adding a specific audience. Document what changed each time.

I.title: Run 2
I.x: Add a tone modifier (e.g. 'conversational but professional')

B.title: Access All Materials

S.n: Copy These Starter Prompts and Make Them Yours

B.x: Use these as starting points. Swap out the placeholders for your real context.

You are a senior [ROLE] with 10 years of experience.
Your task is to [SPECIFIC TASK].

Constraints:
- Keep the response under [WORD COUNT] words
- Use a [TONE] tone
- Format the output as [FORMAT]

Context: [RELEVANT BACKGROUND INFO]

I'm going to show you an example of what I want,
then ask you to create something similar.

Example: [PASTE YOUR EXAMPLE HERE]

Now create a [FORMAT] about [YOUR TOPIC]
that matches the style, length, and tone of my example.

S.n: Now Build Something That Matters to You

B.x: Take everything you've learned today and craft your own AI-powered workflow.
